overview: The Mishnah lists various documents found in a bundle or container that must be returned, and the Gemara discusses the definition of a bundle (אגודה), the validity of a receipt written by the lender (סימפון), and the conditions under which a receipt is valid. — discussion continues into next amud.

sugyaBoundaries.gist: The Mishnah lists various documents found in a bundle or container that must be returned, and the Gemara discusses the definition of a bundle (אגודה), the validity of a receipt written by the lender (סימפון), and the conditions under which a receipt is valid. — discussion continues into next amud.

steps.title: רַב הִסְדָּא's instruction to רַבָּא
steps.stepSummary: Rav Hisda instructs Rabba to examine the halakha, as Rav Huna will ask him about it in the evening.
steps.whatsHappening: רַב הִסְדָּא instructs רַבָּא to examine the halakha. This is a transition to the next discussion.
steps.deeperAnalysis: רַב הִסְדָּא tells רַבָּא to go out and examine the halakha, as רַב הוּנָא will ask him about it in the evening. רַבָּא goes out and finds a relevant source in the מִשְׁנָה. This sets up the discussion about the rule of מַעֲשֵׂה בֵּית דִּין.
steps.whatToRemember: רַב הִסְדָּא's instruction sets up the discussion about the rule of מַעֲשֵׂה בֵּית דִּין.
steps.confusionAlert: The instruction is to examine the halakha, not to make a ruling.
steps.whyThisMatters: The instruction sets up the discussion about the rule of מַעֲשֵׂה בֵּית דִּין.

phrases.english: We are concerned for two Sheviris.

phrases.aramaic: אֲמַר לֵיהּ רַב חִסְדָּא לְרַבָּה: פּוֹק עַיֵּין,
phrases.english: Rav Ḥisda said to Rabba: Go out and examine,

phrases.aramaic: דִּלְאוּרְתָּא בָּעֵי לַהּ רַב הוּנָא מִינָּךְ.
phrases.english: for Rav Huna will ask you about it in the evening.
He went out, examined, and found, as we learned:

phrases.aramaic: כׇּל מַעֲשֵׂה בֵּית דִּין הֲרֵי זֶה יַחְזִיר.
phrases.english: Any enactment of the court, this one returns.

steps.triggerLanguage: אֲמַר לֵיהּ רַב חִסְדָּא לְרַבָּה: פּוֹק עַיֵּין, דִּלְאוּרְתָּא בָּעֵי לַהּ רַב הוּנָא מִינָּךְ.

rashi.hebrew: חיישינן לשני שוירי - אע"פ שבשוירי הידוע לנו אין בה שני יוסף בן שמעון שמא יש שוירי אחרת שיש שם יוסף בן שמעון ושם נכתב ומשליח אחר נפל:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the concern for שני שוירי applies only to the specific שוירי known to us, but if there is another שוירי with a יוסף בן שמעון where the document was written and fell to another messenger, we do not worry about it.

rashi.hebrew: הרי זה יחזיר - דכיון דלא הוחזקו שני יוסף בן שמעון בעיר אחת לעיר אחרת שאינה ידועה לנו לא חיישינן:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that since the two יוסף בן שמעון are not established as being in the same city or in a different unknown city, we do not have to worry about the possibility of confusion.

steps.title: רַב אַמְרָם's challenge: deriving אִסּוּרָא from מָמוֹנָא
steps.stepSummary: Rav Amram challenges Rabba's proof, asking how one can derive a ruling about איסורא (a ritual matter) from a source about ממונא (a monetary matter).
steps.whatsHappening: רַב אַמְרָם challenges רַבָּא's proof. This is a קושיא on the validity of the derivation.
steps.deeperAnalysis: רַב אַמְרָם asks how רַבָּא can derive a ruling about a גֵּט from a מִשְׁנָה that discusses monetary matters. This is a challenge to the validity of רַבָּא's proof. It sets up the discussion about the rule of מַעֲשֵׂה בֵּית דִּין.
steps.whatToRemember: רַב אַמְרָם's challenge is about deriving אִסּוּרָא from מָמוֹנָא.
steps.confusionAlert: The challenge is about the validity of the proof, not about the ruling itself.
steps.whyThisMatters: The challenge sets up the discussion about the rule of מַעֲשֵׂה בֵּית דִּין.

phrases.english: Rav Amram said to Rabba:
How does the Master derive a prohibition from monetary law?

phrases.aramaic: אֲמַר לֵיהּ: תְּרָדָא, ״שְׁטָרֵי חֲלִיצָה וּמֵיאוּנִין״ תְּנַן.
phrases.english: He said to him: "Fool, we learned: 'Documents of halitza and refusal.'"

steps.triggerLanguage: אֲמַר לֵיהּ רַב עַמְרָם לְרַבָּה: הֵיכִי פָּשֵׁיט מָר אִיסּוּרָא מִמָּמוֹנָא?

rashi.hebrew: תרדא - משועמם:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that רבה's response of תרדא means he was annoyed by Rav Amram's question.

tosafot.hebrew: איסורא מממונא - וא"ת טפי אנן מחמירין בממונא דהא אין אנו הולכים בממון אחר הרוב ובאיסורא אזלינן בתר רובא אפי' היכא דאיכא חזקה דאיסורא כנגד הרוב ולא חיישינן שמא במקום נקב קא שחיט וי"ל דמ"מ באשת איש החמירו דמים שאין להם סוף לא תנשא לכתחילה אע"פ שרובם מתים ועוד יש לומר דמדאורייתא לא חיישינן לשני יוסף בן שמעון אבל מדרבנן חיישינן משום לעז ובממונא לא שייך לעז לכך פריך שפיר היכי פשיט מר איסורא מממונא:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות addresses the difficulty that we are stricter in ממונא (following חזקה over רוב) than in איסורא (following רוב over חזקה), and answers that Rav Amram's challenge is valid because we are stricter regarding an אשת איש due to the severity of the prohibition, and also because the concern for two יוסף בן שמעון is only מדרבנן due to לעז, which does not apply to ממונא.

steps.title: The dislodging of the cedar beam
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara relates that the cedar beam of the study hall dislodged, and two sages claim it was due to their fortune.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא relates a story about a cedar beam dislodging from a study hall. This serves as a narrative transition away from the previous halachic debate.
steps.deeperAnalysis: Two sages claim the beam fell due to their personal merit. The story functions as a symbolic or humorous conclusion to the preceding discussion about רב הונא's concern.

phrases.english: The cedar beam of the house of Rav dislodged.

phrases.aramaic: מָר אָמַר: מִשּׁוּם לַתַּאי דִּידִי פְּקַע.
phrases.english: The Master said: Because of my fortune it dislodged.

phrases.aramaic: וּמָר אָמַר: מִשּׁוּם לַתַּאי דִּידִי פְּקַע.
phrases.english: And the Master said: Because of my fortune it dislodged.

steps.triggerLanguage: פְקַע אַרְזָא דְּבֵי רַב. מָר אָמַר: מִשּׁוּם לַתַּאי דִּידִי פְּקַע. וּמָר אָמַר: מִשּׁוּם לַתַּאי דִּידִי פְּקַע.

rashi.hebrew: פקע ארזא דבי רב - נשבר העמוד שבית המדרש נשען עליו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that פקע ארזא דבי רב refers to the breaking of the pillar upon which the בית המדרש was supported.

rashi.hebrew: ומר אמר משום לתאי דידי פקע - שהשבת על דברי לביישני בבית המדרש:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the sages claimed the pillar broke due to their merit because they had previously embarrassed him in the בית המדרש.

steps.title: What is a חֲפִיסָה?
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara asks what is meant by חפיסה.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא asks a clarifying question about the term חֲפִיסָה from the מִשְׁנָה. It seeks a precise definition to understand the law.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The גמרא cites רַבָּא בַּר בַּר חָנָה to explain the term. He defines it as a small flask, clarifying the object in question.

phrases.aramaic: מָצָא בַּחֲפִיסָה אוֹ בִדְלוֹסְקָמָא. מַאי חֲפִיסָה?
phrases.english: Found in a ḥafisa or in a deluskema. What is a ḥafisa?

phrases.aramaic: אָמַר רַבָּה בַּר בַּר חָנָה: חֵמֶת קְטַנָּה.
phrases.english: Rabba bar bar Ḥana said: A small flask.

phrases.aramaic: מַאי דְּלוֹסְקָמָא? אָמַר רַבָּה בַּר שְׁמוּאֵל: טְלִיקָא דְסָבֵי.
phrases.english: What is a deluskema? Rabba bar Shmuel said: A container of the elderly.

steps.triggerLanguage: מַאי חֲפִיסָה?

rashi.hebrew: חמת - של עור שנותנין בו יין:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that חמת is a leather container used for holding wine.

rashi.hebrew: טליקא דסבי - טסקא שהזקנים מצניעים בה כלי תשמישן שלא יצטרכו לחפש אחריהם:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that טליקא דסבי is a small bag in which elderly people hide their personal utensils so they do not need to search for them.

tosafot.hebrew: מצא בחפיסה או בדלוסקמא - תימה בשלמא חפיסה הוי סימן כדאמר בפרק שני (דף כח.) הוא אומר בחפיסה והיא אומרת בחפיסה ינתן לו דמידע ידעה דכל מאי דאית ליה בחפיסה מנח ליה משמע דלאיניש מעלמא הוי סימן אלא דלוסקמא היכי הוי סימן הלא דרך להניח שטרות בדלוסקמא כדתניא בתוספתא דשבועות עשרה דלוסקמאות מלאים שטרות מסרתי לך י"ל דמיירי הכא שנותן בדלוסקמא עצמה סימן שאינו מובהק ולא חיישינן לשאלה לשמא השאיל לאחר ואותו הניח בו שטרות דהכי תקון רבנן דלא חיישינן כמו שתקנו להחזיר אבידה בסימן שאינו מובהק אפילו סימנין לאו דאורייתא:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות questions how a דלוסקמא can serve as a valid sign for returning a lost item, since people commonly place documents in them, and answers that the owner must have placed a non-definitive sign inside the דלוסקמא itself, relying on the rabbinic enactment that we do not worry about the possibility that the item was lent to someone else who placed documents inside it.

steps.title: The Baraita's definition of תְּכִרִּיךְ and אֲגוּדָּה
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara cites a Baraita that defines תכריך and אגודה as three documents rolled or tied together.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא cites a בְּרֵיתָא to define תְּכִרִּיךְ and אֲגוּדָּה. This provides the necessary definitions for the מִשְׁנָה's terms.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The בְּרֵיתָה defines תְּכִרִּיךְ as three documents rolled together. It defines אֲגוּדָּה as three documents tied together, establishing clear categories.

phrases.aramaic: תַּכְרִיךְ שֶׁל שְׁטָרוֹת אוֹ אֲגוּדָּה שֶׁל שְׁטָרוֹת וְכוּ׳.
phrases.english: A roll of documents or a bundle of documents, etc.

phrases.aramaic: תָּנוּ רַבָּנַן: כַּמָּה הוּא תַּכְרִיךְ שֶׁל שְׁטָרוֹת?
phrases.english: The Sages taught: How many is a roll of documents?
Three rolled together.
And how many is a bundle of documents?
Three tied together.

steps.triggerLanguage: תָּנוּ רַבָּנַן: כַּמָּה הוּא תַּכְרִיךְ שְׁטָרוֹת? שְׁלֹשָׁה כְּרוּכִין זֶה בָּזֶה. וְכַמָּה הִיא אֲגוּדָּה שְׁטָרוֹת? שְׁלֹשָׁה קְשׁוּרִין זֶה בָּזֶה.

rashi.hebrew: כרוכין זה בזה - וזהו סימן שהבעלים נותנים בהם זה יכריז מציאה שטרות מצאתי וזה יאמר שלי הם ושלשה הן כרוכין הן זה בזה:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the definition of תכריך as three documents rolled together serves as a סימן, allowing the finder to announce the מציאה and the owner to identify it by stating that three documents are rolled together.

steps.title: Inference: a knot is a סימן
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara infers from the Baraita that a knot can serve as a distinguishing mark (סימן).
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא infers a halachic principle from the בְּרֵיתָה. It suggests a knot can serve as a distinguishing mark.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The גמרא reasons that if a knot identifies the bundle, it functions as a סימן. This implies the owner can claim the item by describing the knot.

phrases.english: You heard from it: a bundle is a sign.

steps.triggerLanguage: שָׁמְעַתְּ מִינַּהּ קֶשֶׁר סִימָן.

rashi.hebrew: ש"מ קשר סימן - שיש קשרים שאינן דומין זה לזה ויאמר כך וכך הוא עשוי דאי לא תימא הכי מאי סימנא איכא הכא ותפשוט מינה בעלמא דקשר סימן ובעיא בעלמא היא ומשני מהכא לא תפשוט דתני רבי חייא ג' כרוכין זה בזה הוא אגודה דכרך הוי סימן דכולי עלמא כרכי שטרא שטרא לעצמו וקושרין אותן יחד וזה כרך שלשתן יחד:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the Gemara infers from this case that a knot can serve as a סימן because different knots have distinct appearances, allowing the owner to describe it, but this inference does not establish a general rule since רבי חייא teaches that a specific type of knot (ג' כרוכין זה בזה) is considered an אגודה, which is a valid סימן for documents, implying that not all knots are universally recognized as distinguishing marks.

steps.title: Rejection: Rabbi Chiya's teaching
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara rejects the inference by citing Rabbi Chiya's teaching that the reference is to three documents rolled together.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא rejects the inference by citing רַבִּי חִיָּה. It offers an alternative explanation for the בְּרֵיתָה.
steps.deeperAnalysis: רַבִּי חִיָּה teaches that the reference is to three documents rolled together. The rolling itself, not the knots, serves as the distinguishing mark.

phrases.aramaic: הָא תָּנֵי רַבִּי חִיָּיא: שְׁלֹשָׁה כְּרוּכִין זֶה בָּזֶה.
phrases.english: Here Rabbi Hiyya taught: Three scrolls rolled one into another.

steps.triggerLanguage: הָא תָּנֵי רַבִּי חִיָּיא: שְׁלֹשָׁה כְּרוּכִין זֶה בָּזֶה.

steps.title: Challenge: If so, תְּכִרִּיךְ and אֲגוּדָּה are identical
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges the rejection by asking why the Mishnah mentions both תכריך and אגודה if they are identical.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא challenges the rejection by questioning the redundancy. It asks why the מִשְׁנָה lists both terms if they mean the same thing.
steps.deeperAnalysis: If both terms refer to three rolled documents, the distinction is meaningless. This challenge forces a re-evaluation of the definitions.

phrases.english: If so, is this a roll?

phrases.aramaic: תַּכְרִיךְ כֹּל חַד וְחַד בְּרֵאשֵׁהּ דְּחַבְרֵיהּ,
phrases.english: A roll: each and every one at the head of its companion,
A bundle: they are thrown upon one another (and rolled).

steps.triggerLanguage: אִי הָכִי, הַיְינוּ תַּכְרִיךְ?

rashi.hebrew: דרמו אהדדי - השכיבן זה על זה אורכו על אורכו של חבירו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that אגודה refers to items placed one on top of the other, lengthwise, distinguishing it from תכריך where each item is wrapped individually.

steps.title: What does the finder proclaim?
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara asks what the finder should proclaim to allow the owner to claim the documents.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא asks a practical question about the proclamation. It seeks to clarify what the finder must announce.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The question focuses on the specific details the finder should mention. This clarifies the procedural requirement for returning the documents.

phrases.english: What does he proclaim, how many?

steps.triggerLanguage: מַאי מַכְרִיז, מִנְיָן?

rashi.hebrew: מאי מכריז - המוצא מאי מכריז:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the Gemara's question מַאי מַכְרִיז refers to what the finder of the documents should proclaim.

rashi.hebrew: מנין - ג' שטרות וזה בא ונותן סימן שכרוכין היו יחד:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that מִנְיָן refers to the three documents mentioned in the Mishnah, and the owner will identify them by the sign that they were rolled together.

steps.title: Challenge: Why three documents?
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges the answer by asking why the Mishnah mentions three documents if two would suffice.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא challenges the previous answer by questioning the number. It asks why three documents are required instead of two.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The challenge questions the necessity of the specific number three. This sets up a discussion on the minimum requirement for a valid bundle.

phrases.aramaic: מַאי אִרְיָא תְּלָתָא אֲפִילּוּ תְּרֵין נָמֵי!
phrases.english: What need is there for three? Even two also!

steps.triggerLanguage: מַאי אִרְיָא תְּלָתָא אֲפִילּוּ תְּרֵין נָמֵי!

rashi.hebrew: אפי' תרי נמי - הואיל ואין צריך לבעלים לתת סימן אלא כריכתן:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that even two documents suffice because the owner only needs to provide the sign of how they were tied (כריכתן), not the content of the documents themselves.

steps.title: Resolution: רַבִּינָא's rule about coins
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara resolves the challenge by citing Ravina's rule about coins.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא resolves the challenge by citing רַבִּינָא's rule about finding coins. This is a setup line introducing the resolution.
steps.deeperAnalysis: רַבִּינָא rules that a finder of coins simply proclaims 'coins' without specifying the number. Here, the finder proclaims 'documents,' and the owner describes them by number and being rolled together. If only two exist, the description is deficient because 'documents' is already plural.

phrases.english: Rather, as Ravina said: The coin proclaims
Here too, "documents" proclaims.

steps.triggerLanguage: אֶלָּא כִּדְאָמַר רָבִינָא: טִבְעָא מַכְרֵיז, הָכָא נָמֵי ״שְׁטָרֵי״ מַכְרֵיז.

rashi.hebrew: כדאמר רבינא - באלו מציאות (דף כה.):
rashi.takeaway: רש״י cites the source of Ravina's rule from the Mishnah in אלו מציאות (דף כה.).

rashi.hebrew: שטרי מכריז - שטרות מצאתי וזה צריך שיאמר כך וכך היו וכרוכין היו הלכך דוקא תלתא אבל תרי מידע ידיע דמיעוט שטרות שתים ואין מנין סימן:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that when proclaiming found documents, the finder says 'I found documents,' and the owner must specify the number and that they were rolled; thus, only three documents constitute a valid sign, whereas two is the minimum plural and therefore not a distinguishing sign.

steps.title: רַבָּן שִׁמְעוֹן בֶּן גַּמְלִיאֵל's rule about loans
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara cites Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel's rule about returning documents of loans to the debtor or creditor.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא cites רַבָּן שִׁמְעוֹן בֶּן גַּמְלִיאֵל's rule about found loan documents. This is a setup line introducing a new ruling.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The rule states that if one finds documents of loans where one person borrowed from three, they are returned to the debtor. If three borrowed from one, they are returned to the creditor. This establishes the baseline for the following discussion.

phrases.english: Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel says:

phrases.aramaic: אֶחָד הַלֹּוֶה מִשְּׁלֹשָׁה יַחְזִיר לַלֹּוֶה וְכוּ׳.
phrases.english: One who borrows from three, returns to the lender etc.
For if your mind enters that they are lenders,
What do they need from each other?

steps.triggerLanguage: רַבָּן שִׁמְעוֹן בֶּן גַּמְלִיאֵל אוֹמֵר: אֶחָד הַלֹּוֶה מִשְּׁלֹשָׁה יַחְזִיר לַלֹּוֶה וְכוּ׳.

steps.title: Challenge: perhaps they went to court
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges the explanation by suggesting that the creditors might have gone to court to ratify their promissory notes.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא challenges the previous explanation by suggesting an alternative scenario. This is a challenge line.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The challenge suggests the creditors might have gone to court to ratify their promissory notes, and the notes were lost together there. This undermines the assumption that the documents' location implies ownership.

phrases.english: Perhaps they went to ratify them? They are ratified.

steps.triggerLanguage: דִּלְמָא לְקַיּוֹמִינְהוּ אָזְלִי?

rashi.hebrew: ודלמא לקיומינהו אזלי - אצל סופר הדיינין הכותב הנפק לכל השטרות:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that לקיומינהו אזלי means the creditors went to a סופר or דיינין who writes and validates all the documents.

steps.title: Resolution: A person does not leave his ratified document with the סוֹפֵר
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara resolves the previous difficulty by stating that a person does not leave his ratified promissory note in the possession of the scribe.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא answers the previous challenge regarding where the documents fell from. This is a resolution line.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The answer is that a person does not leave his ratified document with the סוֹפֵר. This implies the documents were lost by the debtor, not the scribe, preserving the original assumption.

phrases.english: Perhaps something from the scribe fell?

phrases.aramaic: לָא מְשַׁהֵי אִינִישׁ קִיּוּמֵיהּ בִּידָא דְסָפְרָא.
phrases.english: A person does not leave his ratification in the hand of the scribe.

steps.triggerLanguage: דִּלְמָא מִידָא דְסָפְרָא נְפֻל? לָא מְשַׁהֵי אִינִישׁ קִיּוּמֵיהּ בִּידָא דְסָפְרָא.

steps.title: Explanation of the משנה: Why return documents of three borrowers to one lender?
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara explains the Mishnah's ruling that documents of three borrowers from one lender must be returned to the lender.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא introduces the משנה's ruling and explains the reasoning behind it. This is a setup line introducing an explanation.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The משנה rules that if three people borrowed from one person, the documents are returned to the lender. The גמרא explains that if they belonged to the borrowers, they wouldn't be together, implying they belong to the lender.

phrases.aramaic: שְׁלֹשָׁה שֶׁלָּווּ מֵאֶחָד יַחְזִיר לַמַּלְוֶה וְכוּ׳.
phrases.english: Three who borrowed from one — he returns to the lender, etc.
For if your mind enters that they are debtors,
What do they need with each other?

steps.triggerLanguage: שְׁלֹשָׁה שֶׁלָּווּ מֵאֶחָד יַחְזִיר לַמַּלְוֶה וְכוּ׳. דְּאִי סָלְקָא דַעְתָּךְ דְּלֹוִין נִינְהוּ, מַאי בָּעוּ גַבֵּי הֲדָדֵי?

rashi.hebrew: דלווין היו - שפרעום והוחזרו להם:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the Gemara's assumption that the borrowers are the owners (דלווין נינהו) refers to a scenario where they have already repaid the loans and the documents were returned to them.

steps.title: Challenge: Perhaps they went to the סוֹפֵר together to write the documents
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges the previous explanation by suggesting the borrowers went to the scribe together to write the documents.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא raises a difficulty against the assumption that the documents belong to the lender. This is a challenge line.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The גמרא asks if the borrowers went to the סוֹפֵר together to write the documents. This would explain why they are together even if they belong to the borrowers, challenging the previous logic.

keyTerms.meaning: That they are written in three scribes' hands

phrases.english: Perhaps we go to write them?
That they are written in three scribes' hands.

steps.triggerLanguage: דִּלְמָא לְמִכְתְּבִנְהוּ אֲזֻל? דִּכְתִיבִי בִּתְלָת יְדֵי סָפְרֵי.

rashi.hebrew: למכתבנהו אזלי - ומן הסופר נפל ומעולם לא לוו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the Gemara's suggestion that they went to the scribe together implies the documents fell from the scribe's possession and were never actually loaned out.

steps.title: Challenge: Perhaps they went to ratify the documents together
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges the previous resolution by suggesting the borrowers went to ratify the documents together.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא raises another difficulty against the assumption that the documents belong to the lender. This is a challenge line.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The גמרא asks if the borrowers went to ratify the documents together. This would also explain why they are together even if they belong to the borrowers, further challenging the previous logic.

phrases.english: And perhaps we go to establish them?

phrases.aramaic: מַלְוֶה מְקַיֵּים שְׁטָרֵיהּ, לֹוֶה לָא מְקַיֵּים שְׁטָרֵיהּ.
phrases.english: A creditor establishes his promissory notes, a debtor does not establish his promissory notes.

steps.triggerLanguage: וְדִלְמָא לְקַיּוֹמִינְהוּ אֲזֻל? מַלְוֶה מְקַיֵּים שְׁטָרֵיהּ, לֹוֶה לָא מְקַיֵּים שְׁטָרֵיהּ.

steps.title: רַב's ruling: A receipt from the lender's hand is invalid
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara cites Rav's ruling that a receipt from the lender's hand is invalid, even if written in his own handwriting.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא introduces a new topic: the validity of a receipt (סִימְפּוֹן) from the lender's hand. This is a setup line introducing a new ruling.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The גמרא cites רַב's ruling that a receipt from the lender's hand is invalid, even if written in his own handwriting. This is because it might be a joke or written prematurely.

keyTerms.meaning: That emerges from the lender's hand

phrases.aramaic: אִם יֵשׁ עִמָּהֶן סִמְפּוֹנוֹת – יַעֲשֶׂה מָה שֶׁבַּסִּמְפּוֹנוֹת.
phrases.english: If there are cancellations among them – he should do what is in the cancellations.

phrases.aramaic: אָמַר רַב יִרְמְיָה בַּר אַבָּא אָמַר רַב:
phrases.english: Rav Yirmeya bar Abba said, Rav said:
A cancellation that emerges from the hand of a creditor,

phrases.aramaic: אַף עַל פִּי שֶׁכָּתוּב בִּכְתַב יָדוֹ –
phrases.english: even though it is written in his own handwriting –
it is only as joking, and it is invalid.

steps.triggerLanguage: אִם יֵשׁ עִמָּהֶן סִמְפּוֹנוֹת – יַעֲשֶׂה מָה שֶּׁבַּסִּמְפּוֹנוֹת. אָמַר רַב יִרְמְיָה בַּר אַבָּא אָמַר רַב: סִמְפּוֹן הַיּוֹצֵא מִתַּחַת יְדֵי מַלְוֶה, אַף עַל פִּי שֶׁכָּתוּב בְּכַתַּב יָדוֹ – אֵינוֹ אֶלָּא כְּמְשַׂחֵק, וּפָסוּל.

rashi.hebrew: סמפון - שובר המבטל שטר וכל דבר המבטל דבר קרי סמפון כדאמר גבי קדושי אשה ומכירת עבד שהמום קרוי סמפון מפני שמבטל את המקח:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י defines סימפון as any document that nullifies another document, drawing a parallel to the term used for a defect that nullifies a marriage or sale.

rashi.hebrew: היוצא מתחת ידי מלוה - שהוא מונח בידו ולא ביד הלוה:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that 'יוצא מתחת ידי מלוה' means the receipt is in the lender's possession rather than the borrower's.

steps.title: Explanation: The lender might write it prematurely to ensure payment
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara explains Rav's ruling by suggesting the lender might write the receipt prematurely to ensure payment.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא explains the reasoning behind רב's ruling. This is a setup line clarifying the lender's motivation.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The lender fears the borrower might arrive at twilight and refuse payment without a receipt. Therefore, he writes the receipt early to guarantee he gets paid.

keyTerms.meaning: It is not necessary to ask
He thought perhaps he will happen

phrases.aramaic: לָא מִבַּעְיָא כָּתוּב בִּכְתַב יַד סוֹפֵר,
phrases.english: It is not necessary to ask about one written in the handwriting of a scribe,

phrases.aramaic: דְּאִיכָּא לְמֵימַר סָפְרָא אִתְרְמִי לֵיהּ וּכְתַב,
phrases.english: for there is to say that a scribe happened to him and he wrote,

phrases.aramaic: אֶלָּא אֲפִילּוּ כָּתוּב בִּכְתַב יָדוֹ פָּסוּל.
phrases.english: but even if written in his own handwriting it is invalid.

phrases.aramaic: סָבַר דִּלְמָא מִתְרְמֵי וְאָתֵי בֵּין הַשְּׁמָשׁוֹת
phrases.english: He thought perhaps they will happen and come between the evenings
phrases.notes: Refers to the eve of Shabbat, when courts are closed.

phrases.english: and be paying me,

phrases.aramaic: דְּאִי לָא יָהֵיבְנָא לֵיהּ לָא יָהֵיב לִי זוּזֵי,
phrases.english: for if I do not give to him he will not give to me coins,
I will write I that when (I bring) [I bring] to me
coins I will give to him.

steps.triggerLanguage: לָא מִבַּעְיָא כָּתוּב בְּכַתַּב יַד סוֹפֵר, דְּאִיכָּא לְמֵימַר סָפְרָא אִתְרְמִי לֵיהּ וּכְתַב, אֶלָּא אַפִּילּוּ כָּתוּב בְּכַתַּב יָדוֹ פָּסוּל. סָבַר דִּלְמָא מִתְרְמֵי וְאָתֵי בֵּין הַשְּׁמָשׁוֹת וְקָא פָרַע לִי, דְּאִי לָא יָהֵיבְנָא לֵיהּ לָא יָהֵיב לִי זוּזֵי, אֶכְתּוֹב אֲנָא דְּכִי (אַיְיתַי) [מַיְיתֵי] לִי זוּזַי אֶתֵּן לֵיהּ.

rashi.hebrew: ספרא אתרמי ליה - וסבר אם יבא בעל חובי לפרעני ואני אין שטרי עכשיו בידי ואם לא יהא שובר מוכן לי לא יפרע כלום:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the lender writes the receipt prematurely because he fears that if the borrower comes to pay and the lender does not have a receipt ready, the borrower will refuse to pay.

rashi.hebrew: אפי' כתוב בכתב ידו - שיש לומר אם לא שנפרע למה ליה למהר ולכתוב לפני פרעון הואיל ויודע לכתוב:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that even if the receipt is written in the lender's own handwriting, it is invalid because one might argue that the lender had no reason to rush and write it before payment if he knew how to write.

steps.title: Challenge from the Mishnah: If there are receipts, do what they say
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges Rav's ruling with the Mishnah that says if there are receipts, do what they say.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא raises a difficulty against רב's ruling from the משנה. This is a challenge line.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The משנה states that if receipts exist, one should follow their instructions. This implies receipts from the lender's hand are valid, contradicting רב.

keyTerms.meaning: If there are receipts with them
He should do what is in the receipts

phrases.aramaic: תְּנַן: אִם יֵשׁ עִמָּהֶן סִמְפּוֹנוֹת, יַעֲשֶׂה מָה שֶׁבְּסִמְפּוֹנוֹת.
phrases.english: We learned: If there are simponot with them, he should do what is in the simponot.

steps.triggerLanguage: תַּנַּן: אִם יֵשׁ עִמָּהֶן סִמְפּוֹנוֹת, יַעֲשֶׂה מָה שֶּׁבְּסִמְפּוֹנוֹת.

rashi.hebrew: ואם יש עמהן סמפון - והכא במלוה עסקינן דקתני מצא שטר בין שטרותיו כו':
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the Mishnah's ruling about following receipts applies specifically to a מלוה עסק, as indicated by the context of finding a document among one's own papers.

steps.title: Resolution: The Mishnah refers to receipts found among torn documents
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara resolves the challenge by stating the Mishnah refers to receipts found among torn documents.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא answers the challenge by specifying the condition of the receipts. This is a resolution line.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The משנה refers to receipts found among torn documents. These are valid because a lender would not tear up a receipt for an unpaid debt.

keyTerms.meaning: That it was found among torn documents

phrases.english: As Rav Safra said:
That it was found among torn documents,

phrases.aramaic: הָכָא נָמֵי שֶׁמְּצָאוֹ בֵּין שְׁטָרוֹת קְרוּעִין.
phrases.english: Here too, it was found among torn documents.

steps.triggerLanguage: כִּדְאָמַר רַב סָפְרָא: שֶׁנִּמְצָא בֵּין שְׁטָרוֹת קְרוּעִין, הָכָא נָמֵי שֶׁמְּצָאוֹ בֵּין שְׁטָרוֹת קְרוּעִין.

rashi.hebrew: כדאמר רב ספרא - לקמן בשמעתין:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י points to the later discussion in the same sugya where רב ספרא's statement is elaborated.

rashi.hebrew: שנמצא לו - השטר שהשובר יוצא עליו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the שובר found is the receipt corresponding to the שטר that was originally issued.

rashi.hebrew: בין שטרותיו קרועים - והוא אינו קרוע לפיכך סומכין על השובר שמקום השטר מוכיח על השובר שהוא אמת שאם לא נפרע לא היה נותן השטר אצל השטרות קרועין כך שמעתי ולי נראה שנמצא השובר בין השטרות קרועין הלכך איכא למימר שנתקבל החוב והשובר הזה אין צריך למלוה הזה אלא ללוה והלוה שכח ביד זה לפיכך נתנו זה עם השטרות שאין צריכין לו דאי ס"ד מלוה כתבו להיות מוכן לו כשירצה לוה לפרעו לא נתנו עם שטרות שאין צריכין לו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that finding the שובר among torn שטרות indicates the debt was paid, as the lender would not place a valid שטר with torn ones unless it was cancelled, and the borrower likely left the receipt there by mistake.

steps.title: Challenge from a Mishnah: A receipt found among one's documents is valid
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges the previous resolution with a Mishnah that says a receipt found among one's documents is valid.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא raises another difficulty against the resolution. This is a challenge line.
steps.deeperAnalysis: A משנה states a receipt found among one's documents is valid. This implies validity even if not found among torn documents.

keyTerms.term: שְׁטָרוֹ שֶׁל יוֹסֵף בֶּן שִׁמְעוֹן פָּרוּעַ
keyTerms.meaning: The document of יוסף בן שמעון is paid

phrases.aramaic: תָּא שְׁמַע: נִמְצָא לְאֶחָד בֵּין שְׁטָרוֹתָיו שְׁטָרוֹ שֶׁל
phrases.english: Come and hear: One was found among his documents a document of
Yosef ben Shimon repaid –
Their two documents are repaid!

steps.triggerLanguage: תָּא שְׁמַע: נִמְצָא לְאֶחָד בֵּין שְׁטָרוֹתָיו שְׁטָרוֹ שֶׁל יוֹסֵף בֶּן שִׁמְעוֹן פָּרוּעַ – שְׁטָרוֹת שְׁנֵיהֶם פְּרוּעִין!

rashi.hebrew: נמצא בין שטרותיו - מתני' היא בגט פשוט (ב"ב דף קעב.) שני יוסף בן שמעון בעיר אחת ונמצא שובר לאדם אחד בין שטרותיו וכתוב בו שטרו של יוסף בן שמעון שבידי פרוע הוא והיה לו שני שטרות על שניהם:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that this Mishnah is found in בבא בתרא and refers to a גט פשוט (simple receipt) found among documents, where two people named יוסף בן שמעון lived in the same city, and the receipt states that the debt of יוסף בן שמעון in his possession is paid, implying it applies to both debts.

rashi.hebrew: שטרות שניהם פרועין - שכל אחד יכול לומר על שלי נכתב שובר ובבבא בתרא פרכינן בלא שובר נמי הא תנן אין אחד יכול להוציא שטר חוב עליהם ומוקמינן ליה במשולשין בשטר ואין משולשין בשובר והא הכא דיוצא מתחת יד מלוה הוא:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the phrase שטרות שניהם פרועין means each person can claim the receipt was written for their debt, and he notes that in בבא בתרא we challenge this by citing a Mishnah that one cannot produce a debt document against them, resolving it by distinguishing between a document with three witnesses and a receipt without three witnesses, and here the receipt comes from the lender's hand.

steps.title: Resolution: The Mishnah also refers to receipts found among torn documents
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara resolves the challenge by stating the Mishnah also refers to receipts found among torn documents.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא answers the challenge by specifying the condition of the receipts. This is a resolution line.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The משנה also refers to receipts found among torn documents. These are valid because a lender would not tear up a receipt for an unpaid debt.

keyTerms.meaning: That it was found among torn documents

phrases.english: As Rav Safra said:
that it was found among torn documents,

phrases.aramaic: הָכָא נָמֵי שֶׁנִּמְצָא בֵּין שְׁטָרוֹת קְרוּעִין.
phrases.english: here too, it was found among torn documents.

steps.triggerLanguage: כִּדְאָמַר רַב סָפְרָא: שֶׁנִּמְצָא בֵּין שְׁטָרוֹת קְרוּעִין, הָכָא נָמֵי שֶׁנִּמְצָא בֵּין שְׁטָרוֹת קְרוּעִין.

rashi.hebrew: שנמצא - השטר בין השטרות פרועין וכל אחד אמר זהו שטר שלי:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the phrase שנמצא refers to a receipt found among other paid receipts, where each person claims the receipt belongs to them.

steps.title: Challenge from a Mishnah: Orphans swear they didn't find a receipt
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges the previous resolution with a Mishnah that says orphans swear they didn't find a receipt.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא raises another difficulty against the resolution. This is a challenge line.
steps.deeperAnalysis: A משנה states orphans swear they did not find a receipt. This implies a receipt is valid even if not among torn documents.

keyTerms.meaning: An oath that our father did not instruct us
That our father did not say to us

phrases.english: Come and hear: "An oath that our father did not instruct us,
and that our father did not say to us,

phrases.aramaic: וְשֶׁלֹּא מָצָאנוּ בֵּין שְׁטָרוֹתָיו שֶׁל אַבָּא
phrases.english: and that we did not find among our father's documents
that this promissory note is paid."

steps.triggerLanguage: תָּא שְׁמַע: ״שְׁבוּעָה שֶׁלֹּא פְּקָדַנוּ אַבָּא, וְשֶׁלֹּא אָמַר לָנוּ אַבָּא, וְשֶׁלֹּא מָצָאנוּ בֵּין שְׁטָרוֹתָיו שֶׁל אַבָּא שֶׁשְּׁטָר זֶה פָּרוּעַ״.

rashi.hebrew: שבועה שלא פקדנו אבא - משנה היא בשבועות (דף מה.) יתומין מן היתומין לא יפרעו אלא בשבועה ומהו שבועתן שבועה שלא פקדנו אבא בצואת מיתה ושלא אמר לנו קודם לכן ושלא מצינו שובר בין שטרותיו של אבא על שטר זה שיהא פרוע הא אם מצאו סמכינן עליה:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that this Mishnah is found in שבועות and establishes that orphans must swear they did not find a שובר among their father's documents, implying that if they did find one, they would rely on it to avoid payment.

steps.title: Resolution: The Mishnah also refers to receipts found among torn documents
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara resolves the challenge by stating the Mishnah also refers to receipts found among torn documents.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא answers the challenge by specifying the condition of the receipts. This is a resolution line.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The משנה also refers to receipts found among torn documents. These are valid because a lender would not tear up a receipt for an unpaid debt.

keyTerms.meaning: That it was found among torn documents

phrases.aramaic: אָמַר רַב סָפְרָא: שֶׁנִּמְצָא בֵּין שְׁטָרוֹת קְרוּעִין.
phrases.english: Rav Safra said: It was found among torn documents.

steps.triggerLanguage: אָמַר רַב סָפְרָא: שֶׁנִּמְצָא בֵּין שְׁטָרוֹת קְרוּעִין.

steps.title: Challenge from a Baraita: A receipt with witnesses is ratified by its signatories
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges the previous resolution with a Baraita that says a receipt with witnesses is ratified by its signatories.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא raises another difficulty against the resolution. This is a challenge line.
steps.deeperAnalysis: A בריתא states a receipt with witnesses is ratified by its signatories. This implies validity even if not found among torn documents.

keyTerms.meaning: A receipt that has witnesses on it
It is ratified by its signatories

phrases.aramaic: תָּא שְׁמַע: סִמְפּוֹן שֶׁיֵּשׁ עָלָיו עֵדִים יִתְקַיֵּים בְּחוֹתְמָיו.
phrases.english: Come and hear: A simpon that has witnesses upon it is ratified by its signatories.
Say 'is ratified from its signatories'!

steps.triggerLanguage: תָּא שְׁמַע: סִמְפּוֹן שֶׁיֵּשׁ עָלָיו עֵדִים יִתְקַיֵּים בְּחוֹתְמָיו. אֵימָא ״יִתְקַיֵּים מֵחוֹתְמָיו״!

rashi.hebrew: יתקיים בחותמיו - קא ס"ד אם אמר מלוה לא נפרעתי ותדע שהרי לא מסרתי לידך יתקיים בחותמיו ע"פ חותמיו יתקיים אם החותמיו מעידין שחתמוהו אין המלוה נאמן אבל כל כמה דלא מקיים מהימן דמאן מפיק ליה מלוה ומלוה הא אמר פסול הוא:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the Baraita's phrase יתקיים בחותמיו implies a scenario where the lender claims he was not repaid and did not hand over the receipt, so the document is validated by the witnesses' testimony that they signed it, rendering the lender's claim of invalidity ineffective.

tosafot.hebrew: סמפון שיש עליו עדים יתקיים בחותמיו - נראה דמשמע ליה שהוא ביד המלוה מדקרי ליה סמפון דביד הלוה קרי ליה שובר ולשון סמפון לשון ביטול ועוד דביד הלוה פשיטא דכשר ע"י קיום:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות explains that the term סמפון indicates the document is in the lender's possession, as a document in the borrower's hand is called a שובר, and סמפון implies cancellation, whereas validation by witnesses is obvious if it were in the borrower's hand.
